text.title: Internal Link Rotation Schedule Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in solo internal-link rotation cadences. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix keyed to a methodology rule.

antipattern.symptom: By week 6, 2 of 3 scheduled slots have silently slipped; rotation_calendar shows past dates with status='scheduled' (never executed).
antipattern.root-cause: Slots silently rolled forward without explicit pause + reason.
antipattern.fix: Pause the schedule with reason logged (e.g. "operator off-grid week of YYYY-MM-DD") rather than silent slip. Resume at the next available slot. Rule r5.

antipattern.symptom: Deferred-fix register has 14 'open' rows with due_date in the past; new fixes pile on each rotation; nothing ever closes.
antipattern.root-cause: Operator treats deferred fixes as wishlist, not commitments.
antipattern.fix: Escalate overdue rows into the next rotation's first slot. Reduce fixes_deferred per slot to ≤2 if backlog growing. Rule r3.

antipattern.symptom: Audit log shows 60-min slots with fixes_shipped=0 and fixes_deferred=0 — slot executed but nothing changed.
antipattern.root-cause: Operator walks the cluster but defers every decision; audit becomes ritual.
antipattern.fix: Require ≥1 fix_shipped OR ≥1 fix_deferred per audit; otherwise the slot reports "no findings" with an explicit narrative explaining the cluster is healthy.

antipattern.symptom: End of quarter passes with no retrospective; next quarter starts the same rotation order despite known cluster issues.
antipattern.root-cause: Retrospective treated as optional; no calendar trigger.
antipattern.fix: Schedule retrospective in the calendar as a 30-min slot at end of quarter. Validator rejects bundle if quarter is closed AND retrospective.written_at missing. Rule r6.
